title: Differential-expression teaching layer (exercises + notebook)
context: The synthetic differential-expression matrix from ds-expr-013 needs a complete teaching layer to become a usable lesson. The teaching layer includes: defined learning objectives for differential expression analysis, graded exercises (e.g. run a DE test, interpret volcano plot, multiple testing correction), worked solutions, and a runnable Python notebook. The autograding framework from autograde-012 should be used to structure exercises so they are CI-testable. The safety notice must appear in the notebook's first cell. An educator reviewer confirms pedagogy; a technical reviewer confirms the notebook runs correctly.
objective: Produce the teaching layer for the ds-expr-013 synthetic expression matrix: defined learning objectives, graded exercises with worked solutions (using autograde-012 framework), and a runnable Python notebook.
acceptanceCriteria: Learning objectives stated: e.g. 'perform and interpret a differential expression analysis (e.g. DESeq2-equivalent logic or scipy); interpret a volcano plot; apply and explain multiple testing correction (Benjamini-Hochberg)'.
At least 2 graded exercises with complete worked solutions, structured using the autograde-012 framework so autograder can confirm reference solution scores full marks.
Runnable Python Jupyter notebook: first cell contains the mandatory safety notice; notebook runs without errors; instructor notes included.
Educator reviewer confirms the teaching layer teaches the stated DE skill at the stated level.
Technical reviewer confirms the notebook runs and solutions are correct.
Document committed to the project repo alongside ds-expr-013; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: open-teaching-datasets-ds-expr-013 (the expression matrix this lesson uses)
output: A teaching-layer document set (exercises, solutions, runnable Jupyter notebook, instructor note) committed alongside the ds-expr-013 dataset.
